 {"id":"bd-ie5g","title":"UX: Resolution options should mention --autostash flag","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T18:36:24.896204741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T18:46:30.205193055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-06T18:46:30.205193055Z","close_reason":"Fixed: dirty resolution options now show 'ru sync --autostash' as first recommended option","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"bd-ij92","title":"Implement dep-update subcommand skeleton","description":"# Task: dep-update subcommand skeleton

## Interface
```
ru dep-update [OPTIONS]
  --dry-run           Show what would be updated (no changes)
  --manager=NAME      Only update deps for specific manager
  --include=PAT       Only update deps matching pattern
  --exclude=PAT       Skip deps matching pattern
  --major             Include major version updates (default: skip)
  --test-cmd=CMD      Custom test command (overrides detection)
  --max-fix-attempts=N  Max iterations for test/fix loop (default: 5)
  --no-push           Commit but don't push
  --repo=PATH         Single repo mode (default: all repos)
```

## Full Flow
1. For each repo (or single repo if --repo specified):
   a. Detect package managers
   b. Check outdated deps (filter by include/exclude/major)
   c. Fetch changelogs for all outdated deps
   d. Detect test command (or use --test-cmd)
   e. Build full context prompt with:
      - Project understanding (AGENTS.md, README)
      - Outdated deps list
      - Changelog content
      - Test command
   f. Spawn ntm session with Phase 1 prompt (analysis)
   g. Spawn ntm session with Phase 2 prompt (update/test/fix loop)
   h. Wait for completion or timeout
   i. Log results
2. Print summary: updated deps, fixed issues, any failures

## Test/Fix Loop (inside AI session)
- AI updates one dep at a time
- Runs test command after each update
- If tests fail: analyze, fix, re-run (up to max-fix-attempts)
- If tests pass: commit and continue
- If max attempts exceeded: rollback that dep, report failure

## Exit Codes
- 0: All updates successful, all tests passing
- 1: Some deps failed to update (partial success)
- 2: Tests failed after max fix attempts
- 4: Invalid arguments","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:01.932450569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T08:23:57.327962159Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T08:23:57.327962159Z","close_reason":"Implemented full dep-update subcommand with all options: --dry-run, --manager, --include, --exclude, --major, --test-cmd, --max-fix-attempts, --no-push, --repo, --agent, --timeout. Includes helper functions for filtering deps and fetching changelogs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-63u1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:12.575436903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-6k2j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:49.148917241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-g19d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:49.119542826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-jhx3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:07:10.030616179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-ks8z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:25.040695662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-mwuk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:12.572003917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ij92","depends_on_id":"bd-whw3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:04:24.977926159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ilxq","title":"Create Unit Tests for update-manifest.sh Script","description":"# Create Unit Tests for update-manifest.sh Script

## Background

The `update-manifest.sh` script in scoop-bucket is critical infrastructure that:
1. Fetches checksums from GitHub releases
2. Updates version numbers in JSON manifest files
3. Updates SHA256 hashes using jq
4. Handles Scoop-specific URL patterns (including #/ rename syntax)

This script has **NO unit tests** currently. Any bug can corrupt manifests, break Windows installations, and require manual recovery.

## Test Coverage Goals

### 1. JSON Manipulation Tests

```bash
test_version_update_in_json() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    echo '{"version": "0.1.50", "description": "Test tool"}' > "$temp_manifest"
    
    update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "0.1.55"
    
    local new_version=$(jq -r .version "$temp_manifest")
    assert_equals "0.1.55" "$new_version"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}

test_hash_update_in_architecture_block() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    cat > "$temp_manifest" << 'JSON'
{
    "version": "0.1.50",
    "architecture": {
        "64bit": {
            "url": "https://example.com/tool.zip",
            "hash": "old_hash_here"
        }
    }
}
JSON

    update_manifest_hash "$temp_manifest" "64bit" "new_hash_value"
    
    local new_hash=$(jq -r .architecture[\"64bit\"].hash "$temp_manifest")
    assert_equals "new_hash_value" "$new_hash"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}

test_simple_manifest_hash_update() {
    # cm uses simple format without architecture block
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    cat > "$temp_manifest" << 'JSON'
{
    "version": "0.2.3",
    "url": "https://example.com/cm.exe#/cm.exe",
    "hash": "sha256:old_hash"
}
JSON

    update_simple_manifest_hash "$temp_manifest" "new_hash_value"
    
    local new_hash=$(jq -r .hash "$temp_manifest")
    assert_equals "sha256:new_hash_value" "$new_hash"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}
```

### 2. URL Pattern Tests

```bash
test_url_version_replacement() {
    local url="https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/xf/releases/download/v0.1.50/xf-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip"
    local new_url=$(update_url_version "$url" "0.2.0")
    assert_contains "$new_url" "v0.2.0"
    assert_not_contains "$new_url" "v0.1.50"
}

test_autoupdate_url_pattern_valid() {
    # Verify $version placeholder works
    local autoupdate_url="https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/xf/releases/download/v\$version/xf-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip"
    local resolved=$(resolve_autoupdate_url "$autoupdate_url" "0.2.0")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/xf/releases/download/v0.2.0/xf-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip" \
        "$resolved"
}

test_rename_fragment_preserved() {
    # cm.exe uses #/cm.exe rename pattern
    local url="https://github.com/.../cass-memory-windows-x64.exe#/cm.exe"
    local new_url=$(update_url_version "$url" "0.2.4")
    assert_contains "$new_url" "#/cm.exe" "Rename fragment should be preserved"
}
```

### 3. Checksum Format Tests

```bash
test_sha256_prefix_handling() {
    # Scoop uses "sha256:HASH" format
    local raw_hash="abc123def456789"
    local formatted=$(format_scoop_hash "$raw_hash")
    assert_equals "sha256:abc123def456789" "$formatted"
}

test_sha256_prefix_already_present() {
    local hash="sha256:abc123"
    local formatted=$(format_scoop_hash "$hash")
    assert_equals "sha256:abc123" "$formatted" "Should not double-prefix"
}

test_checksum_file_parsing() {
    local checksums="abc123  tool-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip
def456  tool-i686-pc-windows-msvc.zip"
    
    local hash=$(parse_windows_checksum "$checksums" "x86_64")
    assert_equals "abc123" "$hash"
}
```

### 4. JSON Validity Tests

```bash
test_output_is_valid_json() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    cp "cass.json" "$temp_manifest"
    
    update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "99.99.99"
    update_manifest_hash "$temp_manifest" "64bit" "fakehash123"
    
    # Validate JSON syntax
    assert_command_succeeds "jq . $temp_manifest > /dev/null" "Output should be valid JSON"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}

test_preserves_all_fields() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    cat > "$temp_manifest" << 'JSON'
{
    "version": "0.1.0",
    "description": "Test tool",
    "homepage": "https://github.com/test",
    "license": "MIT",
    "custom_field": "should_be_preserved"
}
JSON
    local original_fields=$(jq 'keys | length' "$temp_manifest")
    
    update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "0.2.0"
    
    local new_fields=$(jq 'keys | length' "$temp_manifest")
    assert_equals "$original_fields" "$new_fields" "All fields should be preserved"
    
    local custom=$(jq -r .custom_field "$temp_manifest")
    assert_equals "should_be_preserved" "$custom"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}

test_formatting_matches_scoop_style() {
    # Scoop manifests use 4-space indentation
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    cp "cass.json" "$temp_manifest"
    
    update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "0.1.56"
    
    # Check indentation (should be 4 spaces)
    local indent=$(grep -m1 "description" "$temp_manifest" | sed 's/[^ ].*//')
    assert_equals "    " "$indent" "Should use 4-space indentation"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}
```

### 5. Error Handling Tests

```bash
test_handles_missing_manifest() {
    local result
    result=$(update_manifest_version "/nonexistent/manifest.json" "1.0.0" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error for missing file"
}

test_handles_invalid_json() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    echo "not valid json {{{" > "$temp_manifest"
    
    local result
    result=$(update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "1.0.0" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error for invalid JSON"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}

test_handles_empty_version() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    echo '{"version": "1.0.0"}' > "$temp_manifest"
    
    local result
    result=$(update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should reject empty version"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}

test_handles_missing_architecture_block() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    echo '{"version": "1.0.0"}' > "$temp_manifest"
    
    local result
    result=$(update_manifest_hash "$temp_manifest" "64bit" "abc123" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report missing architecture block"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}
```

### 6. Idempotency Tests

```bash
test_idempotent_version_update() {
    local temp_manifest=$(mktemp --suffix=.json)
    cp "cass.json" "$temp_manifest"
    
    update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "0.1.55"
    local hash1=$(sha256sum "$temp_manifest")
    
    update_manifest_version "$temp_manifest" "0.1.55"
    local hash2=$(sha256sum "$temp_manifest")
    
    assert_equals "$hash1" "$hash2" "Same version update should be idempotent"
    rm "$temp_manifest"
}
```

## Test File Location

`/data/projects/scoop-bucket/tests/unit/test_update_manifest.sh`

## Dependencies

- Requires test framework (bd-p5x1) to be implemented first
- Requires jq to be installed
- Should use temp files, not modify real manifests

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All test functions implemented
- [ ] Tests pass on clean scoop-bucket checkout
- [ ] Tests run in < 20 seconds total
- [ ] Coverage > 80% of update-manifest.sh
- [ ] CI workflow runs tests on every PR
- [ ] JSON logging enabled for CI

## Logging Requirements

Each test logs:
1. Test name with JSON manipulation type
2. Input JSON structure (abbreviated)
3. Expected vs actual JSON values
4. jq query used (for debugging)
5. Duration in milliseconds

 {"id":"bd-ircy","title":"Define phase prompts with structured output markers","description":"# Phase Prompts with Structured Output

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Define the three phase prompts that produce machine-parseable JSON output.

## Phase 1 Prompt (Understanding)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE1_PROMPT='First read AGENTS.md (if present) and README.md (if present) carefully.
If a file is missing, explicitly note that and continue.
Then use your investigation mode to understand the codebase architecture,
entrypoints, conventions, and what the current changes appear to be.
At the end, output a short structured summary as JSON between:
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_BEGIN
{ "summary": "...", "conventions": [...], "risks": [...], "notes": [...] }
RU_UNDERSTANDING_JSON_END'
```

## Phase 2 Prompt (Commit Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT='Now, based on your knowledge of the project, DO NOT run git commands.
Instead, produce a COMMIT PLAN as JSON between these markers:
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_COMMIT_PLAN_JSON_END

Rules:
- Do not edit any code or files.
- Do not include ephemeral/ignored files (.pyc, node_modules, __pycache__, etc.).
- Group changes into logically connected commits.
- For each commit, include:
  - "files": explicit list of paths to stage
  - "message": full commit message (subject + body)
- Include "push": true/false
- Include "excluded_files": list of files excluded and why
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Phase 3 Prompt (Release Plan)

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT='If a release is warranted based on the changes, DO NOT execute release commands.
Produce a RELEASE PLAN as JSON between:
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_BEGIN
{ ... }
RU_RELEASE_PLAN_JSON_END

Include:
- "version": proposed version (or null if no release needed)
- "tag": proposed tag (or null)
- "changelog_entry": text to add to CHANGELOG
- "version_files": files to update with new version
- "checks": actions to verify before release (tests/CI)
Use ultrathink.'
```

## Commit Plan Schema
```json
{
  "commits": [
    {"files": ["path/a", "path/b"], "message": "feat(x): summary\n\nBody..."},
    {"files": ["path/c"], "message": "fix(y): summary\n\nBody..."}
  ],
  "push": true,
  "excluded_files": [
    {"path": "__pycache__/foo.pyc", "reason": "bytecode cache"}
  ],
  "assumptions": ["No breaking changes detected"],
  "risks": ["Large diff in core module"]
}
```

## Release Plan Schema
```json
{
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "tag": "v1.2.0",
  "changelog_entry": "## v1.2.0 (2026-01-06)\n\n### Added\n- ...",
  "version_files": [
    {"path": "VERSION", "old": "1.1.0", "new": "1.2.0"}
  ],
  "checks": ["tests", "lint"]
}
```

AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE1_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 1
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE2_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 2
AGENT_SWEEP_PHASE3_PROMPT="..."  # Override Phase 3

 {"id":"bd-ix9c","title":"Implement run_sequential_agent_sweep()","description":"# Sequential Agent Sweep Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Process repositories one at a time (default mode, safest).

## Implementation

```bash
run_sequential_agent_sweep() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local with_release="$2"
    local success_count=0
    local fail_count=0
    
    for repo_spec in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        local repo_name repo_path session_name
        repo_name=$(basename "$repo_spec" | sed "s/@.*//")
        repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        session_name="ru_sweep_${repo_name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}_$$"
        
        log_step "Processing: $repo_name"
        
        # Run preflight check
        if \! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
            log_warn "  Skipping: $PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"
            write_result "$repo_name" "agent-sweep" "preflight_failed" "0" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
            continue
        fi
        
        if run_single_agent_workflow "$session_name" "$repo_path" "$with_release"; then
            log_success "  Completed: $repo_name"
            ((success_count++))
            mark_repo_completed "$repo_spec"
        else
            log_error "  Failed: $repo_name"
            ((fail_count++))
        fi
        
        save_agent_sweep_state "in_progress"
    done
    
    SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=$success_count
    SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=$fail_count
    
    [[ $fail_count -gt 0 ]] && return 1
    return 0
}
```

## Features
- Preflight check before each repo
- State saved after each repo (for resume)
- Clear progress logging
- Counts success/fail for summary","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:57:06.003069108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T04:37:55.273510603Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T04:37:55.273510603Z","close_reason":"Function fully implemented with progress tracking, success/failure recording, and proper error handling","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-51fm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:58:47.585784474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-b00c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:58:47.557304053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:58:47.606664655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix9c","depends_on_id":"bd-yrod","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T22:55:16.040252145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jhx3","title":"Implement test runner detection","description":"# Task: Test runner detection

## What
Detect the appropriate test command for a repo based on its package manager and config files.

## Detection Rules
| Manager | Check For | Test Command |
|---------|-----------|--------------|
| npm | package.json scripts.test | npm test |
| npm | jest.config.* | npx jest |
| pip | pytest.ini, conftest.py | pytest |
| pip | setup.py | python -m pytest |
| cargo | Cargo.toml | cargo test |
| go | *_test.go files | go test ./... |
| make | Makefile with test target | make test |

## Fallback Priority
1. Check package.json scripts.test (npm/node projects)
2. Check Makefile for test target
3. Check for pytest markers
4. Check for *_test.* files
5. Return empty string if no test runner found

## Interface
`detect_test_command <repo_path>` -> string (test command or empty)

## Output Examples
- `/data/projects/foo` with package.json → `npm test`
- `/data/projects/bar` with Cargo.toml → `cargo test`
- `/data/projects/baz` with no tests → `` (empty)

## Considerations
- Some projects have multiple test commands (unit, integration, e2e)
- Default to the most common/quickest test suite
- Allow override via --test-cmd flag in dep-update","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:07:05.034698229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T08:10:47.433935573Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T08:10:47.433935573Z","close_reason":"Implemented detect_test_command function with support for npm/jest/vitest, cargo, go, pytest, ruby/rspec, maven, gradle, make, and composer","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
-{"id":"bd-jhxw","title":"Create Unit Tests for update-formula.sh Script","description":"# Create Unit Tests for update-formula.sh Script

## Background

The `update-formula.sh` script in homebrew-tap is critical infrastructure that:
1. Fetches checksums from GitHub releases
2. Updates version numbers in Ruby formula files
3. Updates SHA256 hashes for multiple architectures
4. Handles different formula types (simple vs architecture-specific)

This script has **NO unit tests** currently. Any bug in this script can corrupt formulas, break installations for all users, and require manual recovery.

## Test Coverage Goals

### 1. Version Extraction Tests

```bash
test_version_extraction_from_github_api() {
    # Mock GitHub API response
    local mock_response='{"tag_name": "v0.1.55"}'
    local version=$(extract_version "$mock_response")
    assert_equals "0.1.55" "$version" "Should strip v prefix"
}

test_version_extraction_handles_semver() {
    assert_equals "1.2.3" $(extract_version '{"tag_name": "v1.2.3"}')
    assert_equals "0.0.1" $(extract_version '{"tag_name": "v0.0.1"}')
    assert_equals "10.20.30" $(extract_version '{"tag_name": "v10.20.30"}')
}

test_version_extraction_handles_no_v_prefix() {
    local mock_response='{"tag_name": "0.1.55"}'
    local version=$(extract_version "$mock_response")
    assert_equals "0.1.55" "$version" "Should handle tags without v prefix"
}
```

### 2. Checksum Fetching Tests

```bash
test_checksum_fetch_parses_sha256sums_file() {
    # Mock checksum file content
    local mock_checksums="abc123def456  cass-darwin-amd64.tar.gz
789xyz000111  cass-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
fedcba654321  cass-linux-amd64.tar.gz"
    
    local darwin_amd64=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-amd64")
    assert_equals "abc123def456" "$darwin_amd64"
}

test_checksum_fetch_handles_missing_arch() {
    local mock_checksums="abc123  tool-linux-amd64.tar.gz"
    local result=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-arm64")
    assert_equals "" "$result" "Should return empty for missing arch"
}

test_checksum_fetch_network_error_handling() {
    # Simulate network failure
    local result
    result=$(fetch_checksums "https://invalid.example.com/404" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error on network failure"
}
```

### 3. Formula Update Tests

```bash
test_formula_update_changes_version() {
    # Create temp formula file
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cat > "$temp_formula" << 'FORMULA'
class Cass < Formula
  version "0.1.50"
  # ... rest of formula
end
FORMULA

    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local new_version=$(grep 'version' "$temp_formula" | head -1)
    assert_contains "$new_version" "0.1.55"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}

test_formula_update_preserves_structure() {
    # Ensure update doesnt corrupt formula
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cp "Formula/cass.rb" "$temp_formula"
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "99.99.99"
    
    # Validate Ruby syntax
    ruby -c "$temp_formula" 2>&1
    assert_command_succeeds "ruby -c $temp_formula" "Formula should remain valid Ruby"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}

test_formula_update_handles_multi_arch() {
    # Test architecture-specific formula updates
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cat > "$temp_formula" << 'FORMULA'
class Xf < Formula
  on_macos do
    on_intel do
      sha256 "old_hash_intel"
    end
    on_arm do
      sha256 "old_hash_arm"
    end
  end
end
FORMULA

    update_formula_hash "$temp_formula" "darwin-amd64" "new_hash_intel"
    update_formula_hash "$temp_formula" "darwin-arm64" "new_hash_arm"
    
    assert_contains "$(cat $temp_formula)" "new_hash_intel"
    assert_contains "$(cat $temp_formula)" "new_hash_arm"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}
```

### 4. URL Construction Tests

```bash
test_url_construction_for_tar_gz() {
    local url=$(construct_release_url "cass" "0.1.55" "darwin-amd64" "tar.gz")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search/releases/download/v0.1.55/cass-darwin-amd64.tar.gz" \
        "$url"
}

test_url_construction_for_exe() {
    local url=$(construct_release_url "cm" "0.2.3" "windows-x64" "exe")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system/releases/download/v0.2.3/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe" \
        "$url"
}
```

### 5. Edge Case Tests

```bash
test_handles_empty_version() {
    local result
    result=$(update_formula_version "Formula/cass.rb" "" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should reject empty version"
}

test_handles_malformed_checksum() {
    local mock_checksums="not-a-valid-checksum-line"
    local result=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-amd64")
    assert_equals "" "$result" "Should handle malformed checksum gracefully"
}

test_handles_formula_not_found() {
    local result
    result=$(update_formula_version "/nonexistent/formula.rb" "1.0.0" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error for missing formula"
}

test_idempotent_update() {
    # Running update twice with same version should be safe
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cp "Formula/cass.rb" "$temp_formula"
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local hash1=$(sha256sum "$temp_formula")
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local hash2=$(sha256sum "$temp_formula")
    
    assert_equals "$hash1" "$hash2" "Same update should be idempotent"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}
```

### 6. Logging Tests

```bash
test_logs_include_timestamp() {
    LOG_FORMAT=json ./update-formula.sh cass 0.1.55 --dry-run 2>&1 | head -1 > /tmp/log_line.json
    assert_command_succeeds "jq -e .timestamp /tmp/log_line.json" "Log should include timestamp"
}

test_logs_include_tool_name() {
    LOG_FORMAT=json ./update-formula.sh cass 0.1.55 --dry-run 2>&1 | head -1 > /tmp/log_line.json
    local tool=$(jq -r .tool /tmp/log_line.json)
    assert_equals "cass" "$tool" "Log should include tool name"
}
```

## Test File Location

`/data/projects/homebrew-tap/tests/unit/test_update_formula.sh`

## Dependencies

- Requires test framework (bd-p5x1) to be implemented first
- Uses mock data/fixtures instead of real network calls where possible
- Should run in CI and locally

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All test functions implemented
- [ ] Tests pass on clean homebrew-tap checkout
- [ ] Tests run in < 30 seconds total
- [ ] Coverage report shows > 80% line coverage of update-formula.sh
- [ ] CI workflow runs tests on every PR
- [ ] Tests use JSON logging in CI mode

## Detailed Logging Requirements

Each test MUST log:
1. Test name and description
2. Input values/fixtures used
3. Expected vs actual results
4. Duration in milliseconds
5. Any cleanup actions taken

```bash
test_url_construction_for_tar_gz() {
    local url=$(construct_release_url "cass" "0.1.55" "darwin-amd64" "tar.gz")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search/releases/download/v0.1.55/cass-darwin-amd64.tar.gz" \
        "$url"
}

test_url_construction_for_exe() {
    local url=$(construct_release_url "cm" "0.2.3" "windows-x64" "exe")
    assert_equals \
        "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system/releases/download/v0.2.3/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe" \
        "$url"
}
```

### 5. Edge Case Tests

```bash
test_handles_empty_version() {
    local result
    result=$(update_formula_version "Formula/cass.rb" "" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should reject empty version"
}

test_handles_malformed_checksum() {
    local mock_checksums="not-a-valid-checksum-line"
    local result=$(parse_checksum "$mock_checksums" "darwin-amd64")
    assert_equals "" "$result" "Should handle malformed checksum gracefully"
}

test_handles_formula_not_found() {
    local result
    result=$(update_formula_version "/nonexistent/formula.rb" "1.0.0" 2>&1) || true
    assert_contains "$result" "ERROR" "Should report error for missing formula"
}

test_idempotent_update() {
    # Running update twice with same version should be safe
    local temp_formula=$(mktemp)
    cp "Formula/cass.rb" "$temp_formula"
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local hash1=$(sha256sum "$temp_formula")
    
    update_formula_version "$temp_formula" "0.1.55"
    local hash2=$(sha256sum "$temp_formula")
    
    assert_equals "$hash1" "$hash2" "Same update should be idempotent"
    rm "$temp_formula"
}
```

### 6. Logging Tests

```bash
test_logs_include_timestamp() {
    LOG_FORMAT=json ./update-formula.sh cass 0.1.55 --dry-run 2>&1 | head -1 > /tmp/log_line.json
    assert_command_succeeds "jq -e .timestamp /tmp/log_line.json" "Log should include timestamp"
}

test_logs_include_tool_name() {
    LOG_FORMAT=json ./update-formula.sh cass 0.1.55 --dry-run 2>&1 | head -1 > /tmp/log_line.json
    local tool=$(jq -r .tool /tmp/log_line.json)
    assert_equals "cass" "$tool" "Log should include tool name"
}
```

## Test File Location

`/data/projects/homebrew-tap/tests/unit/test_update_formula.sh`

## Dependencies

- Requires test framework (bd-p5x1) to be implemented first
- Uses mock data/fixtures instead of real network calls where possible
- Should run in CI and locally

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All test functions implemented
- [ ] Tests pass on clean homebrew-tap checkout
- [ ] Tests run in < 30 seconds total
- [ ] Coverage report shows > 80% line coverage of update-formula.sh
- [ ] CI workflow runs tests on every PR
- [ ] Tests use JSON logging in CI mode

## Detailed Logging Requirements

Each test MUST log:
1. Test name and description
2. Input values/fixtures used
3. Expected vs actual results
4. Duration in milliseconds
5. Any cleanup actions taken

 {"id":"bd-jk4n","title":"[EPIC] Security Guardrails & Validation","description":"# Security Guardrails & Validation

## Critical Insight
The prompt "Don't commit ephemeral files" is NOT sufficient. The agent can ignore it.
ru MUST enforce guardrails before executing any commit/push.

## Planner → Validator → Executor Model
1. Agent produces structured JSON plans (commits, releases)
2. ru validates plans against security rules
3. ru executes only validated plans
4. Agent NEVER directly runs git commands

## File Denylist
Before executing commit plan, validate each file against denylist:
- .env, .env.*
- *.pem, *.key, id_rsa, *.p12, *.pfx
- credentials.json, secrets.json
- node_modules, __pycache__, .pyc
- dist/, build/, *.log, .DS_Store

## File Size Limits
Default: 10MB max per file (configurable via AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB)

## Binary Detection
Detect binary files and require explicit allow:
- Uses `file -b` to check if file is text/script/json/xml

## Secret Scanning
Layered approach:
1. If gitleaks installed: Full scan
2. Elif detect-secrets installed: Scan
3. Else: Heuristic patterns (AWS keys, GitHub PATs, private keys, etc.)

Block push if secrets detected. Write artifact report. Mark repo failed.

## Commit Plan Validation
validate_commit_plan() checks:
- Files not on denylist
- Files under size limit
- No binaries without explicit allow
- Secret scan passes

## Release Plan Validation
- Version format valid
- Version files exist
- Tag format valid

## Policy
Default: Block commit on validation failure, preserve plan in artifacts for manual review.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:45:36.238165636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T05:31:22.167759123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T05:31:22.167759123Z","close_reason":"All implementation tasks completed - features working and tested","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jk4n","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T21:59:20.089190203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

Fix:
- Ensure get_repo_status always outputs numeric ahead/behind (use -1/-1 for unknown).
- Avoid leaking a global output var by making it local.
- Add a regression test that forces rev-list failure via a git wrapper and asserts AHEAD/BEHIND are numeric.

Acceptance:
- ru status --json prints valid JSON even when rev-list fails.
- scripts/test_local_git.sh includes a passing regression test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T20:17:58.069291577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T05:06:53.025462672Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T05:06:53.025462672Z","close_reason":"Fixed: AHEAD/BEHIND now use -1 for unknown (numeric for JSON). Added local output var. Regression tests added.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver
- Common code calls driver_* functions
- Drivers can extend interface with driver-specific features
- Capabilities check enables graceful feature degradation

## Testing
- Mock driver for unit tests
- Verify interface consistency between drivers
- Test driver selection logic
- Verify event normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interface defined with all required operations
- [ ] Driver selection works correctly
- [ ] Event schema documented and consistent
- [ ] Capabilities query implemented
- [ ] Driver loading mechanism works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T20:35:14.284949263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:39:51.577905888Z","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:39:51.577905888Z","close_reason":"Unified driver interface implemented with: load_review_driver(), driver_capabilities(), driver_start_session(), driver_send_to_session(), driver_get_session_state(), driver_stop_session(), driver_interrupt_session(), driver_stream_events(), driver_list_sessions(), driver_session_alive(). Includes event schema documentation.
